arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- If a live state immediately reconnects to a known solved lane within a handful of steps, treat it as classification work, not progress.
- If a checkpoint family has produced multiple rejoin proofs from different-looking microstates, mark the whole family stale instead of preserving each microstate as promising.
- Scheduled self-improvement turns should explicitly convert repeated rediscoveries into playbook/todo/memory updates so later action turns stop burning probes on the same family.
- When a branch is genuinely fresh, make the branchpoint checkpoint the important one; deeper live states should usually be rewound into that checkpoint before serious probing.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach.
- Best reset checkpoints now:
  - `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`
  - `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`
  - `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`
  - `turn368_forest_25_24_postbattle`
  - `turn346_forest_18_1_toprow_fresh`
  - `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn325_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn309_forest_17_5_newdrop`
  - `turn299_forest_16_12_start`
  - `turn297_forest_18_13_loaded`
  - `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
  - `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
  - `turn279_forest_18_7_visible`
  - `turn278_forest_18_9_visible`
  - `turn277_forest_18_13_visible`
- Avoid `boundary_t250` for normal progress unless specifically testing battle recovery.
- `handoff_t250` is also not a useful progress reset: it loads into a wild battle and, after escape, only returns to stale `(20,8)` upper-connector geometry.
- Live `(18,6)` is effectively stale now; do not spend a normal action turn there.
- Live `(14,32)` is part of the newly found west-side extension from the `(17,40)` family; next action should prefer rewinding to `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` or `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`, not blindly continuing from a deeper live tile without a fresh checkpoint.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Verified east-side climb
- From roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`, north to about `(25,30)`, then straight north corridor to `(25,22)` and `(25,10)`.
- Near top, east is mostly blocked except a small shift; shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`.

### Newer genuinely different branch family: lower-mid / west sign-row approach
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` is currently the most promising non-stale forest family.
- From `(17,40)`, `down` is blocked; the real lane goes north to `(17,32)`.
- From this family, a lateral probe reached a new fork at `(18,35)`; save/use `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` as the main exploration reset.
- From that fork, west/north travel reached `(14,32)` and as far left as `(11,32)`.
- Repeated `up` at the far-left end around `(11,32)` was blocked, so the branch is not just a straight hidden climb there.
- Treat `(18,35)` as the decision point for the next serious probe: test alternate moves there before grinding the already-tested far-left end.

### Stale family: trainer/sign pocket and east wall
- Rejoin markers: if a probe settles around `(30,18)` or `(26,30)` with the Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby, treat it as solved geometry and rewind.
- `(25,18)`, `(26,20)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(31,24)`, and east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)` are all stale entries into the same solved trainer/sign pocket.
- The sign at `(25,17)` is only `TRAINER TIPS`.
- East-edge south corridor from `(31,17)` down to `(32,28..33)` loops back northwest into the same pocket.
- `turn271_forest_32_28_start` / `(32,28)` is stale too: `down x5, left, up x4` lands at `(31,29)` with the familiar lower trainer pocket visible; further left at `(31,33)` is blocked.
- Live `(26,22)` north continuation is stale too: from `turn367_forest_26_22_postrun`, going north rejoins `(25,18)` / the familiar sign pocket.

### Stale family: upper-west / top row
- From upper-east area around `(27,8)`, west reaches the lane around `(24,8)` and eventually `(18,7)`.
- From `(18,7)`, north climbs to top row around `(17..18,1)`.
- Top row facts: left only to `(16,1)` before blocking; right is open far across to at least `(32,1)`.
- Tested columns `x=21..28` behave as `(x,1) -> (x,2)` with second `down` blocked.
- Far-right descent `(32,1) -> south` rejoins `(30,18)`.
- Far-east lower-strip continuation from `(27,2)` also rejoins `(30,18)`.
- The center-left `(18,1)` descent is real but stale: it rejoins solved sign-pocket / upper-connector geometry.
- The diagonal variation from `(18,1)` also rejoins `(17,5)/(17,6)` upper connector.
- Alternating `right` and blocked `up` across the top row found no hidden openings.
- The far-right stair-step descent `(29,1)->(32,7)` is stale east-wall/top-row geometry.
- The farther-right snake variation ending near `(32,2)` is also stale solved top-row/east-wall geometry.

### Stale family: upper connector / upper-west microloops
- From `(20,8)`, `left x4 -> (16,8)`, `up x2 -> (16,6)`, `right x2 -> (18,6)`.
- A third `right` from `(18,6)` is blocked.
- From `(18,6)`, probing `up, up, left, left, up` reaches `(16,1)`.
- The rectangle `(18,6) -> (18,4) -> (16,4) -> (16,6) -> (18,6)` is just a small local loop.
- Longer south/southwest descent from `(18,6)` ultimately lands at `(14,18)`, i.e. the mid-west pocket.
- `A` interaction checks around suspicious upper-connector tiles produced no hidden object/sign/NPC.
- Live `(19,8)` and its immediate right-side square are only a tiny local connector loop.
- `turn252_post_kakuna_16_8` / `(16,8)` is also stale: `right,right,up,right(blocked),down,right,up(blocked),up(blocked),left,up,right(blocked),up` loops through `(18,8)`, `(19,8)`, `(18,7)` and ends back at `(18,6)`.
- Live `(17,5)` is stale too; right-side probes rejoin `(19,3)` and the same top-center connector system.
- `handoff_t250` is effectively part of this stale family too: after escaping the opening Weedle battle it resumes at `(20,8)`.

### Stale family: south continuation / mid-west pocket
- Around `(18,9)`, `up` is blocked.
- `left,left` reaches `(16,9)`; `down,down` reaches `(16,11)`.
- `right,right` reaches `(18,11)`, but further right is blocked.
- From `(17,11)`, going down eventually enters the mid-west pocket.
- Pocket around `(11..14,15..19)` is a self-contained loop.
- Older checkpoint `turn258_forest_15_17_start` is also stale: it quickly loops through `(13,15)`, `(13,13)`, `(11,13)`, and `(11,18)` with left/right blocks, i.e. the same mid-west family.
- Treat `(16,12)`, `(16,10)`, `(16,5)`, `(17,5)`, `(18,12)`, `(18,14)`, and similar nearby states as stale members of this solved family.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(19,8)` / `turn344_forest_19_8_fresh`; it is a tiny local loop.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)` / `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh` unless doing narrowly targeted classification; rewind first.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, or east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`; rewind first.
- Do **not** treat the far-right top-row descent or the `(27,2)` lower-strip east-wall descent as fresh progress.
- Do **not** overinvest in `(16,12)` / `(16,10)` / `(18,12)` / `(16,5)` / `(17,5)` / `(18,14)` / `turn258_forest_15_17_start`; they reconnect to solved upper-west/top-row or mid-west systems.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`, `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`, then the older classification checkpoints only if specifically comparing families.
- If repeated rewinds from the new `(17,40)/(18,35)` family also collapse into classified systems, stop doing adjacent micro-probes and consider leaving the forest route entirely to reset perspective.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial (like a lone `G`), assume it is still scrolling; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely without overmashing.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`, `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`, `turn368_forest_25_24_postbattle`, `turn265_forest_26_20_start`, `turn269_forest_25_22_start`, `turn269_forest_28_18_pretrainer`, `turn269_forest_31_19_trainer_side`, `turn277_forest_18_13_visible`, `turn279_forest_18_7_visible`, `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`, `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`, `turn297_forest_18_13_loaded`, `turn299_forest_16_12_start`, `turn309_forest_17_5_newdrop`, `turn325_forest_18_6_fresh`, `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh`, `turn346_forest_18_1_toprow_fresh`.

data.result:
---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.

# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

### Treat as **known loop / solved geometry** if any of these are true
- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.
- You are around **(26,30)** or **(27,30)** with the lower-right Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby.
- You descended the **far-right top row** from around `(32,1)` and are now moving south on the east wall.
- You are in the **mid-west pocket** around `(11..14,15..19)`.
- You are back near the **upper-west lane** around `(18,7)`, `(18,9)`, `(18,11)`, or `(20,8)` without having found a map change or clearly unseen corridor.
- A route from your current state matches one of these known reconnections:
  - `(25,23) -> up x5, right x2 -> (27,18)`
  - `(32,1) -> down to east wall -> south -> (30,18)`
  - east-edge south corridor eventually returns to `(27,30)` / `(30,18)`
  - `(20,8)` eastward connector returns to the known upper-east corridor near `(25,11)`

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true
- You found a **new map change**.
- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

Preferred rewind order:
1. `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
2. `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
3. `turn2

data.result:
Route 2 north-side Bug Catcher + battle timing notes: the overworld trainer around `(26,33)` in the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor pocket is hostile and starts a Bug Catcher battle when talked to. Battle sequence observed: first enemy Weedle Lv6, then Caterpie Lv6. Squirtle leveled to 10 during the fight. Important battle handling lessons from this trainer:

- Visible move order in Squirtle's move list is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on Tail Whip, `down, A` selects Bubble.
- Blind multi-inputs are risky; one mistake used Tail Whip on Caterpie.
- After `used BUBBLE!`, the game can sit on the move text/animation for a long time. The reliable pattern was **wait first, then use single A presses with generous settle**, not repeated mash.
- Enemy move/result text can chain across multiple boxes and look odd (for example Weedle text resolving as `attack missed! But, it failed!`). Treat these as ordinary text boxes and advance carefully.
- Trainer battles on this approach should be handled manually rather than with wild flee/mash routines.

Live state at start of turn 130: still in the same trainer battle versus Caterpie Lv6, Squirtle Lv10 at 9/30 HP, command menu visible.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- If a live state immediately reconnects to a known solved lane within a handful of steps, treat it as classification work, not progress.
- If a checkpoint family has produced multiple rejoin proofs from different-looking microstates, mark the whole family stale instead of preserving each microstate as promising.
- Scheduled self-improvement turns should explicitly convert repeated rediscoveries into playbook/todo/memory updates so later action turns stop burning probes on the same family.
- When a branch is genuinely fresh, make the branchpoint checkpoint the important one; deeper live states should usually be rewound into that checkpoint before serious probing.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes that may page/loop text awkwardly.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach.
- Best reset checkpoints now:
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
  - `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`
  - `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`
  - `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`
  - `turn368_forest_25_24_postbattle`
  - `turn346_forest_18_1_toprow_fresh`
  - `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn325_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn309_forest_17_5_newdrop`
  - `turn299_forest_16_12_start`
  - `turn297_forest_18_13_loaded`
  - `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
  - `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
  - `turn279_forest_18_7_visible`
  - `turn278_forest_18_9_visible`
  - `turn277_forest_18_13_visible`
- Avoid `boundary_t250` for normal progress unless specifically testing battle recovery.
- `handoff_t250` is also not a useful progress reset: it loads into a wild battle and, after escape, only returns to stale `(20,8)` upper-connector geometry.
- Live `(18,6)` is effectively stale now; do not spend a normal action turn there.
- Lower forest around `(25,33) -> (27,43)` is now the freshest real progress family and should be prioritized over older sign-row retries unless specifically comparing families.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Verified east-side climb
- From roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`, north to about `(25,30)`, then straight north corridor to `(25,22)` and `(25,10)`.
- Near top, east is mostly blocked except a small shift; shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`.

### Freshest family now: lower trainer area discovered from the `(26,24)` corridor
- The old `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor` family was not just a stale rejoin: escaping the Kakuna around `(25,27)` and continuing south opened a real lower-area continuation.
- Key progression chain so far:
  - around `(25,27)` walk straight south to about `(25,33)`
  - this opens a broader lower forest with trainers/signs around `(27,34)` and `(27,43)`
  - from `(25,34)`, a lower lane continues through `(25,35)` toward live `(27,38)`
- Important checkpoints:
  - `turn375_forest_25_27_grasscorridor`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
- Practical rule: treat this lower area as best current progress. Save before trainer interaction or grass crossings.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression by itself.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.
- Live `(27,38)` with visible Bug Catcher below is part of the fresh lower-area family; next action should map the safe lanes around that trainer before doing random grass dives.

### Newer genuinely different branch family: lower-mid / west sign-row approach
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` is still a genuinely different forest family, but it is no longer the top priority while the lower trainer area remains fresh.
- From `(17,40)`, `down` is blocked; the real lane goes north to `(17,32)`.
- From this family, a lateral probe reached a new fork at `(18,35)`; save/use `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` as the main exploration reset for that family.
- From that fork, west/north travel reached `(14,32)` and as far left as `(11,32)`.
- Repeated `up` at the far-left end around `(11,32)` was blocked, so the branch is not just a straight hidden climb there.
- The southward continuation near `(15,37)` is a dead-end/wild-battle wedge, not a breakthrough.

### Stale family: trainer/sign pocket and east wall
- Rejoin markers: if a probe settles around `(30,18)` or `(26,30)` with the Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby, treat it as solved geometry and rewind.
- `(25,18)`, `(26,20)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(31,24)`, and east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)` are all stale entries into the same solved trainer/sign pocket.
- The sign at `(25,17)` is only `TRAINER TIPS`.
- East-edge south corridor from `(31,17)` down to `(32,28..33)` loops back northwest into the same pocket.
- `turn271_forest_32_28_start` / `(32,28)` is stale too: `down x5, left, up x4` lands at `(31,29)` with the familiar lower trainer pocket visible; further left at `(31,33)` is blocked.
- Live `(26,22)` north continuation is stale too: from `turn367_forest_26_22_postrun`, going north rejoins `(25,18)` / the familiar sign pocket.
- Lower-area branch from `(27,34)` to `(31,29)` is also stale east-wall rejoin; do not keep following that route.

### Stale family: upper-west / top row
- From upper-east area around `(27,8)`, west reaches the lane around `(24,8)` and eventually `(18,7)`.
- From `(18,7)`, north climbs to top row around `(17..18,1)`.
- Top row facts: left only to `(16,1)` before blocking; right is open far across to at least `(32,1)`.
- Tested columns `x=21..28` behave as `(x,1) -> (x,2)` with second `down` blocked.
- Far-right descent `(32,1) -> south` rejoins `(30,18)`.
- Far-east lower-strip continuation from `(27,2)` also rejoins `(30,18)`.
- The center-left `(18,1)` descent is real but stale: it rejoins solved sign-pocket / upper-connector geometry.
- The diagonal variation from `(18,1)` also rejoins `(17,5)/(17,6)` upper connector.
- Alternating `right` and blocked `up` across the top row found no hidden openings.
- The far-right stair-step descent `(29,1)->(32,7)` is stale east-wall/top-row geometry.
- The farther-right snake variation ending near `(32,2)` is also stale solved top-row/east-wall geometry.

### Stale family: upper connector / upper-west microloops
- From `(20,8)`, `left x4 -> (16,8)`, `up x2 -> (16,6)`, `right x2 -> (18,6)`.
- A third `right` from `(18,6)` is blocked.
- From `(18,6)`, probing `up, up, left, left, up` reaches `(16,1)`.
- The rectangle `(18,6) -> (18,4) -> (16,4) -> (16,6) -> (18,6)` is just a small local loop.
- Longer south/southwest descent from `(18,6)` ultimately lands at `(14,18)`, i.e. the mid-west pocket.
- `A` interaction checks around suspicious upper-connector tiles produced no hidden object/sign/NPC.
- Live `(19,8)` and its immediate right-side square are only a tiny local connector loop.
- `turn252_post_kakuna_16_8` / `(16,8)` is also stale: `right,right,up,right(blocked),down,right,up(blocked),up(blocked),left,up,right(blocked),up` loops through `(18,8)`, `(19,8)`, `(18,7)` and ends back at `(18,6)`.
- Live `(17,5)` is stale too; right-side probes rejoin `(19,3)` and the same top-center connector system.
- `handoff_t250` is effectively part of this stale family too: after escaping the opening Weedle battle it resumes at `(20,8)`.

### Stale family: south continuation / mid-west pocket
- Around `(18,9)`, `up` is blocked.
- `left,left` reaches `(16,9)`; `down,down` reaches `(16,11)`.
- `right,right` reaches `(18,11)`, but further right is blocked.
- From `(17,11)`, going down eventually enters the mid-west pocket.
- Pocket around `(11..14,15..19)` is a self-contained loop.
- Older checkpoint `turn258_forest_15_17_start` is also stale: it quickly loops through `(13,15)`, `(13,13)`, `(11,13)`, and `(11,18)` with left/right blocks, i.e. the same mid-west family.
- Treat `(16,12)`, `(16,10)`, `(16,5)`, `(17,5)`, `(18,12)`, `(18,14)`, and similar nearby states as stale members of this solved family.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(19,8)` / `turn344_forest_19_8_fresh`; it is a tiny local loop.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)` / `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh` unless doing narrowly targeted classification; rewind first.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, or east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`; rewind first.
- Do **not** treat the far-right top-row descent or the `(27,2)` lower-strip east-wall descent as fresh progress.
- Do **not** overinvest in `(16,12)` / `(16,10)` / `(18,12)` / `(16,5)` / `(17,5)` / `(18,14)` / `turn258_forest_15_17_start`; they reconnect to solved upper-west/top-row or mid-west systems.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, then `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`, and `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`.
- If the lower trainer area also collapses into classified systems, then return to branch-comparison mode and reconsider the older `(17,40)/(18,35)` family.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial (like a lone `G`), assume it is still scrolling; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely without overmashing.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`, `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`, `turn368_forest_25_24_postbattle`, `turn265_forest_26_20_start`, `turn269_forest_25_22_start`, `turn269_forest_28_18_pretrainer`, `turn269_forest_31_19_trainer_side`, `turn277_forest_18_13_visible`, `turn279_forest_18_7_visible`, `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`, `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`, `turn297_forest_18_13_loaded`, `turn299_forest_16_12_start`, `turn309_forest_17_5_newdrop`, `turn325_forest_18_6_fresh`, `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh`, `turn346_forest_18_1_toprow_fresh`.

arguments.content: Viridian Forest turn 384: from checkpoint `turn381_forest_15_40_west_npc`, probing `up, up` reached `(15,38)` and immediately triggered a wild Metapod. After a slow battle-text progression and successful RUN, the overworld resumed at `(17,40)` facing right. This strongly suggests the western lower-area NPC pocket around `(15,40)` can rejoin the older `(17,40)` corridor family through grass/battle disruption rather than opening a clean new dry-road breakthrough. Treat the direct north probe from `(15,40)` as risky and likely stale unless testing a very specific alternate lane.

# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- If a live state immediately reconnects to a known solved lane within a handful of steps, treat it as classification work, not progress.
- If a checkpoint family has produced multiple rejoin proofs from different-looking microstates, mark the whole family stale instead of preserving each microstate as promising.
- Scheduled self-improvement turns should explicitly convert repeated rediscoveries into playbook/todo/memory updates so later action turns stop burning probes on the same family.
- When a branch is genuinely fresh, make the branchpoint checkpoint the important one; deeper live states should usually be rewound into that checkpoint before serious probing.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes that may page/loop text awkwardly.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach.
- Best reset checkpoints now:

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial (like a lone `G`), assume it is still scrolling; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- Against useless early wilds like Metapod/Kakuna, accidental Tackle while waiting for the menu is survivable; just continue with paced text and run when the 4-option menu finally appears.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- If a live state immediately reconnects to a known solved lane within a handful of steps, treat it as classification work, not progress.
- If a checkpoint family has produced multiple rejoin proofs from different-looking microstates, mark the whole family stale instead of preserving each microstate as promising.
- Scheduled self-improvement turns should explicitly convert repeated rediscoveries into playbook/todo/memory updates so later action turns stop burning probes on the same family.
- When a branch is genuinely fresh, make the branchpoint checkpoint the important one; deeper live states should usually be rewound into that checkpoint before serious probing.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes that may page/loop text awkwardly.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach.
- Best reset checkpoints now:
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
  - `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`
  - `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`
  - `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor`
  - `turn368_forest_25_24_postbattle`
  - `turn346_forest_18_1_toprow_fresh`
  - `turn341_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn325_forest_18_6_fresh`
  - `turn309_forest_17_5_newdrop`
  - `turn299_forest_16_12_start`
  - `turn297_forest_18_13_loaded`
  - `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
  - `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
  - `turn279_forest_18_7_visible`
  - `turn278_forest_18_9_visible`
  - `turn277_forest_18_13_visible`
- Avoid `boundary_t250` for normal progress unless specifically testing battle recovery.
- `handoff_t250` is also not a useful progress reset: it loads into a wild battle and, after escape, only returns to stale `(20,8)` upper-connector geometry.
- Live `(18,6)` is effectively stale now; do not spend a normal action turn there.
- Lower forest around `(23,43) -> (30..32,41)` is now the freshest real progress family and should be prioritized over older sign-row retries.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Verified east-side climb
- From roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`, north to about `(25,30)`, then straight north corridor to `(25,22)` and `(25,10)`.
- Near top, east is mostly blocked except a small shift; shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`.

### Freshest family now: lower open lane into east rim
- The old `turn366_forest_26_24_corridor` family was not just a stale rejoin: escaping the Kakuna around `(25,27)` and continuing south opened a real lower-area continuation.
- Key progression chain so far:
  - around `(25,27)` walk straight south to about `(25,33)`
  - this opens a broader lower forest with trainers/signs around `(27,34)` and `(27,43)`
  - from `(25,34)`, a lower lane continues through `(25,35)` toward live `(27,38)`
  - from `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`
  - from `(23,43)` an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`
- Important checkpoints:
  - `turn375_forest_25_27_grasscorridor`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
- Practical rule: treat this lower/east-rim area as best current progress. Save before trainer interaction or grass crossings.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression by itself.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.
- Live `(27,38)` with visible Bug Catcher below is part of the fresh lower-area family; next action should map safe lanes before random grass dives.

### Lower open-hub / east-rim notes

# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.

data.result:
---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

description: Decide quickly whether a Viridian Forest state is fresh progress or a known loop, and choose the best rewind target instead of re-probing solved pockets.

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

### Treat as **known loop / solved geometry** if any of these are true
- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.
- You are around **(26,30)** or **(27,30)** with the lower-right Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby.
- You descended the **far-right top row** from around `(32,1)` and are now moving south on the east wall.
- You are in the **mid-west pocket** around `(11..14,15..19)`.
- You are back near the **upper-west lane** around `(18,7)`, `(18,9)`, `(18,11)`, or `(20,8)` without having found a map change or clearly unseen corridor.
- A route from your current state matches one of these known reconnections:
  - `(25,23) -> up x5, right x2 -> (27,18)`
  - `(32,1) -> down to east wall -> south -> (30,18)`
  - east-edge south corridor eventually returns to `(27,30)` / `(30,18)`
  - `(20,8)` eastward connector returns to the known upper-east corridor near `(25,11)`

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true
- You found a **new map change**.
- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

Preferred rewind order:
1. `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
2. `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
3. `turn2

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.

- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, or sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`; rewind first.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, then `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, then `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely without overmashing.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

data.result:
---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- Lower forest around `(23,43) -> (30..32,41)` is still the freshest real progress family.
- Current live self-improvement area is the east-pocket / signfork neighborhood around `(28..30,40..42)`, but treat tiny local grass-edge loops as local geometry only.

## Key route notes

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true
- You found a **new map change**.
- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

2. `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- Lower forest around `(23,43) -> (30..32,41)` is still the freshest real progress family.
- New secondary fresh lead: from the lower open lane, a dry-road west/north route reaches `(21,40)`; treat this as a genuinely different branch to probe before returning to known east-rim microloops.
- Current live self-improvement area is the lower open-lane / west-north branch family around `(23,43) -> (21,40)`, with east-rim tiny-step mapping as backup.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Verified east-side climb
- From roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`, north to about `(25,30)`, then straight north corridor to `(25,22)` and `(25,10)`.
- Near top, east is mostly blocked except a small shift; shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`.

### Freshest family now: lower open lane into east rim and west-north offshoot
- Around `(25,27)`, walking south opens real lower-area continuation to `(25,33)` and beyond.
- Important progression chain so far:
  - `(25,27) -> (25,33)` opens the lower forest.
  - `(25,34) -> (27,38)` is a real lower-lane continuation.
  - From `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, a genuinely different west/north dry-road probe reaches `(21,40)`.
- Best checkpoints now:
  - `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`
  - `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`
  - `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
- Practical rule: treat this lower/east-rim area plus the new `(21,40)` west-north offshoot as best current progress. Save before trainer interaction or grass crossings.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- New turn-397 refinement: from `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, probing north via `(26,38) -> (26,36) -> (25,35) -> (26,34) -> (25,34)` just rejoins the already-known lower trainer-visible family. Do not keep re-probing north from `(25,40)`.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.

### Lower open-hub / east-rim notes
- From `(27,38)`, the dry-road loop `left,left,down,down,down,right,right,down` safely reaches `(27,42)`.
- West from that safe lane opens a real hub around `(22,42)` / `(22,43)`, but further west mostly loops into the known lower sign/NPC pocket.
- From the hub checkpoint `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, an east-heavy route reaches `(30,41)` and then `(32,41)`.
- Verified local geometry at the far east rim:
  - `(32,41)` exists and `down` there is blocked.
  - `up` from `(32,41)` works to `(32,40)`.
  - `left,left` from `(32,41)` returns to `(30,41)`.
  - At `(30,41)`, `down` once seemed blocked from one angle, but a different adjacent sequence found continuation: `(30,41) -> down -> (30,42)` and `left -> (29,42)` are both real.
  - At `(30,40)`, `up` is blocked.
  - At `(30,40)`, `right` works to `(31,40)` and `left` returns.
  - At `(29,40)`, `up` is blocked, `left` is blocked, and `A` facing up does nothing.
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge` tiny probe rejoined the signfork family by turn 408; do not overinvest there without a clearly different angle.

### Lower open-hub / west-north offshoot notes
- New dry-road route from `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`:
  - `left, left, up, up, right, right, up, left, left`
  - reaches `(21,40)`.
