### Signfork / lower sign-NPC lane notes
- `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork` is useful for classifying the dry-road tiles just west/south of the sign and NPC lane.
- From `(25,40)`, the northward route is stale and rejoins `(25,34)` family; avoid it.
- From the nearby post-Kakuna state at `(24,41)`, the safe local loop is:
  - `(24,41) -> (25,41) -> (26,41) -> (26,40) -> (25,40) -> (25,41)`
- This means `(24..26,41)` plus `(25..26,40)` is at least a small dry-road pocket with no immediate hidden interaction.
- Treat this signfork pocket as local geometry classification, not a major new branch unless a probe leaves this loop without rejoining the known `(25,34)` or lower-NPC families.

### Secondary fresh family: lower-mid / west sign-row approach
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` is still a genuinely different family, but it is now tertiary work behind the lower open-lane west/north offshoot and the lower east-rim family.
- From `(17,40)`, `down` is blocked; the real lane goes north to `(17,32)`.
- From that family, a lateral probe reached a fork at `(18,35)`; use `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` as the main exploration reset for it.
- The southward continuation near `(15,37)` is a dead-end/wild-battle wedge, not a breakthrough.
- The newer lower clearing around `(15..18,44..46)` climbs back up to `(18,42)` and appears to rejoin known lanes rather than open new progress.

### Stale-family reminders
- If a probe settles around `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, `(31,29..33)`, `(31,8)..(32,11)`, `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, or the mid-west pocket `(11..14,15..19)`, treat it as solved/stale and rewind.
- Top-row experiments from `(18,1)` / `(32,1)` are stale solved geometry.
- `turn271_forest_32_28_start` is stale too.
- Live sign/NPC-lane states around `(26,39)` are stale local loops; rewind rather than continuing there.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, or sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`; rewind first.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, then `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, then `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- Lower forest around `(23,43) -> (30..32,41)` is still the freshest real progress family.
- Current live fallback family being re-checked is the older sign-row / upper-left corridor around `(18,35)` because multiple lower/east branches have rejoined known loops.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Verified east-side climb
- From roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`, north to about `(25,30)`, then straight north corridor to `(25,22)` and `(25,10)`.
- Near top, east is mostly blocked except a small shift; shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`.

### Freshest family now: lower open lane into east rim and west-north offshoot
- Around `(25,27)`, walking south opens real lower-area continuation to `(25,33)` and beyond.
- Important progression chain so far:
  - `(25,27) -> (25,33)` opens the lower forest.
  - `(25,34) -> (27,38)` is a real lower-lane continuation.
  - From `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, a genuinely different west/north dry-road probe reaches `(21,40)`.
- Best checkpoints now:
  - `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`
  - `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`
  - `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`
  - `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
- Practical rule: treat the lower/east-rim area plus the sign-row retry as best current work. Save before trainer interaction or grass crossings.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- New turn-397 refinement: from `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, probing north via `(26,38) -> (26,36) -> (25,35) -> (26,34) -> (25,34)` just rejoins the already-known lower trainer-visible family. Do not keep re-probing north from `(25,40)`.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.

### Lower open-hub / east-rim notes
- From `(27,38)`, the dry-road loop `left,left,down,down,down,right,right,down` safely reaches `(27,42)`.
- West from that safe lane opens a real hub around `(22,42)` / `(22,43)`, but further west mostly loops into the known lower sign/NPC pocket.
- From the hub checkpoint `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, an east-heavy route reaches `(30,41)` and then `(32,41)`.
- Verified local geometry at the far east rim:
  - `(32,41)` exists and `down` there is blocked.
  - `up` from `(32,41)` works to `(32,40)`.
  - `left,left` from `(32,41)` returns to `(30,41)`.
  - At `(30,41)`, `down` once seemed blocked from one angle, but a different adjacent sequence found continuation: `(30,41) -> down -> (30,42)` and `left -> (29,42)` are both real.
  - At `(30,40)`, `up` is blocked.
  - At `(30,40)`, `right` works to `(31,40)` and `left` returns.
  - At `(29,40)`, `up` is blocked, `left` is blocked, and `A` facing up does nothing.
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge` tiny probe rejoined the signfork family by turn 408; do not overinvest there without a clearly different angle.
- Grass on this east rim can trigger encounters while testing return paths; prefer tiny stepwise probes from `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, or `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside` and rewind quickly if it collapses into known lower lanes.

### Lower open-hub / west-north offshoot
- Dry-road route from `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`:
  - `left, left, up, up, right, right, up, left, left`
  - reaches `(21,40)`.
- But the live/post-battle descendants there quickly rejoin the known lower hub around `(21,43)` / `(22,42)`; do not overinvest.

### Sign-row / upper-left retry family
- Turn 417 fresh mapping from `turn415_forest_18_35_retry`:
  - `(18,35) -> (18,34) -> (17,34) -> (16,34) -> (16,35) -> (15,35) -> (15,34) -> (15,33) -> (14,33)` is real.
  - `down` from `(14,33)` is blocked.
  - `(14,33) -> left -> (13,33) -> up -> (13,32) -> right -> (14,32) -> right -> (15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` is also real.
- Caution: this sits near the stale sign-row choke and may reconnect to the known `(12,32)` family. Use only tiny probes and rewind if it collapses back to `(12,32)` or the antidote-sign lane.

### Secondary fresh family: lower-mid / west sign-row approach
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` is still a genuinely different family, but it is now tertiary work behind the lower open-lane family and the turn-417 sign-row retry.
- From `(17,40)`, `down` is blocked; the real lane goes north to `(17,32)`.
- From that family, a lateral probe reached a fork at `(18,35)`; use `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` or `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2` as the main exploration reset for it.
- The southward continuation near `(15,37)` is a dead-end/wild-battle wedge, not a breakthrough.
- The newer lower clearing around `(15..18,44..46)` climbs back up to `(18,42)` and appears to rejoin known lanes rather than open new progress.

### Stale-family reminders

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`, or post-battle sign-row rejoin `(12,32)`; rewind first.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.

- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

### Treat as **known loop / solved geometry** if any of these are true
- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.
- You are around **(26,30)** or **(27,30)** with the lower-right Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby.

- You are back near the **upper-west lane** around `(18,7)`, `(18,9)`, `(18,11)`, or `(20,8)` without having found a map change or clearly unseen corridor.

  - `(25,23) -> up x5, right x2 -> (27,18)`
  - `(32,1) -> down to east wall -> south -> (30,18)`
  - east-edge south corridor eventually returns to `(27,30)` / `(30,18)`
  - `(20,8)` eastward connector returns to the known upper-east corridor near `(25,11)`

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true

- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- Current best live lead is no longer the stale far east rim or upper-left sign-row retry; it is the upper corridor descended from `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, currently known to reach live state `(27,26)`.
- Lower forest around `(23,43) -> (30..32,41)` is still the main solved fresh-family hub, but many sub-branches there now rejoin known loops quickly.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.

### Verified east-side climb

### Freshest family now: upper corridor descended from lower trainer-visible branch

- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`, `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn426_forest_31_7_upper`, `turn426_forest_31_15_live`, `turn425_forest_27_21_retry`, `turn423_forest_27_21_upper_opening`, `turn422_forest_27_26_retry`, `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`, `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.
- Then wait through the white transition back to overworld.

# Recovery rules

## If automation or inputs misfire and the menu is wrong

- Use `B` to back out toward the main 4-option menu.
- From the 4-option menu, ret

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- The former best upper lead from `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible` has now collapsed: the `(27,26) -> (27,21) -> (31,15) -> (31,7)` family ultimately rejoins solved top-row and trainer-pocket geometry.
- Treat live `(30,18)` as a stale rejoin into the old trainer/sign-pocket family, not an active lead.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Former upper-corridor family is now stale
- From roughly `(27,34)`, the path through `(27,26)`, `(27,21)`, `(31,15)`, and `(31,7)` is real movement but **not** progression.
- Verified collapse points:
  - north/east continuation from `(31,15)` rejoins the solved top-row strip around `(28..22,1..2)`.
  - a different west/down branch from `(31,7)` eventually descends to live `(30,18)`, which is the old trainer/sign-pocket rejoin.
- Practical rule: do not spend live action turns from `(31,7)`, `(22,8)`, `(22,9)`, `(22,2)`, or `(30,18)`; rewind instead.

### Lower open lane into east rim and west-north offshoot
- Around `(25,27)`, walking south opens real lower-area continuation to `(25,33)` and beyond.
- Important progression chain so far:
  - `(25,27) -> (25,33)` opens the lower forest.
  - `(25,34) -> (27,38)` is a real lower-lane continuation.
  - From `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, a genuinely different west/north dry-road probe reaches `(21,40)`.
- Best checkpoints now:
  - `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`
  - `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`
  - `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`
  - `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`
  - `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
- Important refinement: `turn419_forest_27_34_retry` is still useful as a rewind anchor, but its promoted upper descendants are no longer fresh.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- New turn-397 refinement: from `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, probing north via `(26,38) -> (26,36) -> (25,35) -> (26,34) -> (25,34)` just rejoins the already-known lower trainer-visible family. Do not keep re-probing north from `(25,40)`.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.
- Turn-419 correction/refinement: from the same `(27,34)` family, not every up/right probe is stale immediately; however, the broader upper-corridor family has now been shown to collapse into old top-row or trainer-pocket geometry. Use this checkpoint only for short branch tests, not long optimism.

### Lower open-hub / east-rim notes
- From `(27,38)`, the dry-road loop `left,left,down,down,down,right,right,down` safely reaches `(27,42)`.
- West from that safe lane opens a real hub around `(22,42)` / `(22,43)`, but further west mostly loops into the known lower sign/NPC pocket.
- From the hub checkpoint `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, an east-heavy route reaches `(30,41)` and then `(32,41)`.
- Verified local geometry at the far east rim:
  - `(32,41)` exists and `down` there is blocked.
  - `up` from `(32,41)` works to `(32,40)`.
  - `left,left` from `(32,41)` returns to `(30,41)`.
  - At `(30,41)`, `down` once seemed blocked from one angle, but a different adjacent sequence found continuation: `(30,41) -> down -> (30,42)` and `left -> (29,42)` are both real.
  - At `(30,40)`, `up` is blocked.
  - At `(30,40)`, `right` works to `(31,40)` and `left` returns.
  - At `(29,40)`, `up` is blocked, `left` is blocked, and `A` facing up does nothing.
  - Turn-418 refinement: `(29,41) -> (29,40) -> (30,40) -> (31,40)` is real, but `up` is blocked at both `(31,40)` and `(30,40)`; treat that as local geometry only.
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge` tiny probe rejoined the signfork family by turn 408; do not overinvest there without a clearly different angle.
- Grass on this east rim can trigger encounters while testing return paths; prefer tiny stepwise probes from `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, or `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside` and rewind quickly if it collapses into known lower lanes.

### Lower open-hub / west-north offshoot
- Dry-road route from `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`:
  - `left, left, up, up, right, right, up, left, left`
  - reaches `(21,40)`.
- But the live/post-battle descendants there quickly rejoin the known lower hub around `(21,43)` / `(22,42)`; do not overinvest.

### Sign-row / upper-left retry family
- Turn 417 fresh mapping from `turn415_forest_18_35_retry`:
  - `(18,35) -> (18,34) -> (17,34) -> (16,34) -> (16,35) -> (15,35) -> (15,34) -> (15,33) -> (14,33)` is real.
  - `down` from `(14,33)` is blocked.
  - `(14,33) -> left -> (13,33) -> up -> (13,32) -> right -> (14,32) -> right -> (15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` is also real.
- Caution: this sits near the stale sign-row choke and may reconnect to the known `(12,32)` family. Use only tiny probes and rewind if it collapses back to `(12,32)` or the antidote-sign lane.

### Secondary/tertiary family reminders
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` and descendants are now tertiary work behind the lower open-lane family and any still-untried micro-branches from older checkpoints.
- The newer lower clearing around `(15..18,44..46)` climbs back up to `(18,42)` and appears to rejoin known lanes rather than open new progress.

### Stale-family reminders
- If a probe settles around `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, `(31,29..33)`, `(31,8)..(32,11)`, `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, or the mid-west pocket `(11..14,15..19)`, treat it as solved/stale and rewind.
- Top-row experiments from `(18,1)` / `(32,1)` are stale solved geometry.
- `turn271_forest_32_28_start` is stale too.
- Live sign/NPC-lane states around `(26,39)` are stale local loops; rewind rather than continuing there.
- The post-Weedle state at `(12,32)` is also stale; it belongs to the known sign-row family and should be rewound out of immediately.
- Live east-rim states `(32,41)` and `(31,40)` are stale wedges; do not spend live action turns there.
- Live upper-family descendants `(22,8)`, `(22,9)`, `(22,2)`, and `(30,18)` are also stale; do not continue from them.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`, post-battle sign-row rejoin `(12,32)`, east-rim wedges `(32,41)` / `(31,40)`, or upper-family wedges `(22,8)` / `(22,9)` / `(22,2)`; rewind first.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: favor lower-area checkpoints like `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, then `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`; use `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2` / `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` only as tertiary alternatives.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- When an apparent fresh family has already produced two different rejoin proofs, demote it aggressively in the playbook/todos instead of continuing to hope.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`, `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

data.result:
---
name: viridian-forest-wild-run-recipe
description: Safely escape common Viridian Forest wild battles by identifying the battle phase first, then using the correct cadence instead of blind mashing.
---
# Purpose

Use this when a wild battle starts in Viridian Forest and the goal is to preserve HP and return to exploration, especially when early-game text/animation lag makes menu timing unreliable.

# Preconditions

- This is a **wild** battle, not a trainer battle.
- Preferred when Squirtle is healthy enough to survive a mistaken turn but you still want to avoid wasting HP/time.
- Best for Caterpie / Weedle / Kakuna / Metapod / similar early wilds.

# Core rule

Do **not** choose RUN until you have identified which battle phase is currently on screen.

There are three common substates:

1. **Intro text** — e.g. `Wild WEEDLE appeared!`
2. **Send-out text / animation lag** — e.g. `Go! A!` or the brief state around it
3. **Visible 4-option command menu** — `FIGHT / PKMN / ITEM / RUN`

Treat them differently.

# Procedure

## Phase 1: Intro text (`Wild X appeared!`)

- Press `A` once.
- Wait briefly.
- Re-check state.
- Do **not** send RUN inputs yet.

Reason: RUN inputs during intro can be ignored or land in the wrong state later.

## Phase 2: Send-out text / animation lag (`Go! A!`)

- Usually wait first rather than mashing.
- Then use a **single** `A` press.
- Wait again if needed.
- Repeat only as needed until the 4-option menu is clearly up.

Reason: this is the most dangerous mis-timing zone. Inputs here can accidentally produce an attack once the menu appears.

## Phase 3: Visible 4-option menu

- Choose RUN explicitly.
- Reliable input from the default FIGHT position: `down, right, A`.
- After `Got away safely!`, press `A` once more if needed.

---
name: viridian-forest-loop-triage
description: Decide quickly whether a Viridian Forest state is fresh progress or a known loop, and choose the best rewind target instead of re-probing solved pockets.
---
# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

### Treat as **known loop / solved geometry** if any of these are true
- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.
- You are around **(26,30)** or **(27,30)** with the lower-right Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby.
- You descended the **far-right top row** from around `(32,1)` and are now moving south on the east wall.
- You are in the **mid-west pocket** around `(11..14,15..19)`.
- You are back near the **upper-west lane** around `(18,7)`, `(18,9)`, `(18,11)`, or `(20,8)` without having found a map change or clearly unseen corridor.
- A route from your current state matches one of these known reconnections:
  - `(25,23) -> up x5, right x2 -> (27,18)`
  - `(32,1) -> down to east wall -> south -> (30,18)`
  - east-edge south corridor eventually returns to `(27,30)` / `(30,18)`
  - `(20,8)` eastward connector returns to the known upper-east corridor near `(25,11)`

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true
- You found a **new map change**.
- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

Preferred rewind order:
1. `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
2. `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
3. `turn2

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- The former best upper lead from `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible` has now collapsed: the `(27,26) -> (27,21) -> (31,15) -> (31,7)` family ultimately rejoins solved top-row and trainer-pocket geometry.
- Treat live `(30,18)` as a stale rejoin into the old trainer/sign-pocket family, not an active lead.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Former upper-corridor family is now stale
- From roughly `(27,34)`, the path through `(27,26)`, `(27,21)`, `(31,15)`, and `(31,7)` is real movement but **not** progression.
- Verified collapse points:
  - north/east continuation from `(31,15)` rejoins the solved top-row strip around `(28..22,1..2)`.
  - a different west/down branch from `(31,7)` eventually descends to live `(30,18)`, which is the old trainer/sign-pocket rejoin.
- Practical rule: do not spend live action turns from `(31,7)`, `(22,8)`, `(22,9)`, `(22,2)`, or `(30,18)`; rewind instead.

### Lower open lane into east rim and west-north offshoot
- Around `(25,27)`, walking south opens real lower-area continuation to `(25,33)` and beyond.
- Important progression chain so far:
  - `(25,27) -> (25,33)` opens the lower forest.
  - `(25,34) -> (27,38)` is a real lower-lane continuation.
  - From `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, a genuinely different west/north dry-road probe reaches `(21,40)`.
- Best checkpoints now:
  - `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`
  - `turn434_forest_28_40_retry`
  - `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`
  - `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`
  - `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`
  - `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`
  - `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`
  - `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`
  - `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`
- Important refinement: `turn419_forest_27_34_retry` is still useful as a rewind anchor, but its promoted upper descendants are no longer fresh.

### Lower trainer-area local notes
- At about `(27,41)`, further `up` is blocked by a Bug Catcher NPC; talking to him begins flavor text `I ran...` and is not hidden progression.
- The sign around `(25,40)` is only advice about avoiding battles by staying out of grass.
- New turn-397 refinement: from `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, probing north via `(26,38) -> (26,36) -> (25,35) -> (26,34) -> (25,34)` just rejoins the already-known lower trainer-visible family. Do not keep re-probing north from `(25,40)`.
- From `(27,34)`, an east/up climb can reach `(31,29)`, but this is stale: it rejoins the already-solved east-wall / trainer-pocket family.
- Turn-419 correction/refinement: from the same `(27,34)` family, not every up/right probe is stale immediately; however, the broader upper-corridor family has now been shown to collapse into old top-row or trainer-pocket geometry. Use this checkpoint only for short branch tests, not long optimism.
- Turn-434 directive lesson: live lower-lane state `(25,36)` with fence/grass strip on the left is not a place to grind tiny probes; rewind to a cleaner lower-area checkpoint instead.

### Lower open-hub / east-rim notes
- From `(27,38)`, the dry-road loop `left,left,down,down,down,right,right,down` safely reaches `(27,42)`.
- West from that safe lane opens a real hub around `(22,42)` / `(22,43)`, but further west mostly loops into the known lower sign/NPC pocket.
- From the hub checkpoint `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, an east-heavy route reaches `(30,41)` and then `(32,41)`.
- Verified local geometry at the far east rim:
  - `(32,41)` exists and `down` there is blocked.
  - `up` from `(32,41)` works to `(32,40)`.
  - `left,left` from `(32,41)` returns to `(30,41)`.
  - At `(30,41)`, `down` once seemed blocked from one angle, but a different adjacent sequence found continuation: `(30,41) -> down -> (30,42)` and `left -> (29,42)` are both real.
  - At `(30,40)`, `up` is blocked.
  - At `(30,40)`, `right` works to `(31,40)` and `left` returns.
  - At `(29,40)`, `up` is blocked, `left` is blocked, and `A` facing up does nothing.
  - Turn-418 refinement: `(29,41) -> (29,40) -> (30,40) -> (31,40)` is real, but `up` is blocked at both `(31,40)` and `(30,40)`; treat that as local geometry only.
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge` tiny probe rejoined the signfork family by turn 408; do not overinvest there without a clearly different angle.
  - Turn 434 new result: from `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, the route `right,right,down,down,left,down,right,right` reaches lower southeast edge `(31,43)`, but this too collapses back into the stale east-rim family: `right -> (32,43)` exists, `down` there is blocked, and `up/up/right/down/left/left` from nearby tiles returns to `(30,41)` / `(32,41)` geometry.
- Grass on this east rim can trigger encounters while testing return paths; prefer tiny stepwise probes from `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, or `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim` and rewind quickly if it collapses into known lower lanes.

### Lower open-hub / west-north offshoot
- Dry-road route from `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`:
  - `left, left, up, up, right, right, up, left, left`
  - reaches `(21,40)`.
- But the live/post-battle descendants there quickly rejoin the known lower hub around `(21,43)` / `(22,42)`; do not overinvest.

### Sign-row / upper-left retry family
- Turn 417 fresh mapping from `turn415_forest_18_35_retry`:
  - `(18,35) -> (18,34) -> (17,34) -> (16,34) -> (16,35) -> (15,35) -> (15,34) -> (15,33) -> (14,33)` is real.
  - `down` from `(14,33)` is blocked.
  - `(14,33) -> left -> (13,33) -> up -> (13,32) -> right -> (14,32) -> right -> (15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` is also real.
- Caution: this sits near the stale sign-row choke and may reconnect to the known `(12,32)` family. Use only tiny probes and rewind if it collapses back to `(12,32)` or the antidote-sign lane.

### Secondary/tertiary family reminders
- `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` and descendants are now tertiary work behind the lower open-lane family and any still-untried micro-branches from older checkpoints.
- The newer lower clearing around `(15..18,44..46)` climbs back up to `(18,42)` and appears to rejoin known lanes rather than open new progress.

### Stale-family reminders
- If a probe settles around `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, `(31,29..33)`, `(31,8)..(32,11)`, `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, or the mid-west pocket `(11..14,15..19)`, treat it as solved/stale and rewind.
- Top-row experiments from `(18,1)` / `(32,1)` are stale solved geometry.
- `turn271_forest_32_28_start` is stale too.
- Live sign/NPC-lane states around `(26,39)` are stale local loops; rewind rather than continuing there.
- The post-Weedle state at `(12,32)` is also stale; it belongs to the known sign-row family and should be rewound out of immediately.
- Live east-rim states `(32,41)` and `(31,40)` are stale wedges; do not spend live action turns there.
- Live upper-family descendants `(22,8)`, `(22,9)`, `(22,2)`, and `(30,18)` are also stale; do not continue from them.

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`, post-battle sign-row rejoin `(12,32)`, east-rim wedges `(32,41)` / `(31,40)`, or upper-family wedges `(22,8)` / `(22,9)` / `(22,2)`; rewind first.
- Preferred fresh-branch fallback order is now: favor lower-area checkpoints like `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, then `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`; use `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2` / `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork` only as tertiary alternatives.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- When an apparent fresh family has already produced two different rejoin proofs, demote it aggressively in the playbook/todos instead of continuing to hope.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`, `turn434_forest_28_40_retry`, `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`, `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.

# Viridian Forest loop triage

Use this when you are in Viridian Forest and suspect the current state may already belong to solved geometry.

## Goal
Avoid wasting turns on repeatedly rediscovering the same pockets, top-row drops, and sign/trainer rejoins.

## Immediate classification checklist

### Treat as **known loop / solved geometry** if any of these are true
- You are around **(30,18)** in the mid-east sign/trainer hub.
- You are around **(26,30)** or **(27,30)** with the lower-right Bug Catcher/sign pocket visible nearby.
- You descended the **far-right top row** from around `(32,1)` and are now moving south on the east wall.
- You are in the **mid-west pocket** around `(11..14,15..19)`.
- You are back near the **upper-west lane** around `(18,7)`, `(18,9)`, `(18,11)`, or `(20,8)` without having found a map change or clearly unseen corridor.
- A route from your current state matches one of these known reconnections:
  - `(25,23) -> up x5, right x2 -> (27,18)`
  - `(32,1) -> down to east wall -> south -> (30,18)`
  - east-edge south corridor eventually returns to `(27,30)` / `(30,18)`
  - `(20,8)` eastward connector returns to the known upper-east corridor near `(25,11)`

### Treat as **potentially fresh** only if at least one is true
- You found a **new map change**.
- You reached coordinates not already documented in the forest notes.
- You found a corridor that does **not** quickly reconnect to `(30,18)`, `(26,30)`, the upper-west lane, or the mid-west pocket.
- You have a clean checkpoint right before probing and can test a short branch safely.

## Rewind policy
If the current state is classified as a known loop, rewind instead of exploring outward.

Preferred rewind order:
1. `turn287_forest_18_6_postbattle`
2. `turn286_forest_20_8_visible`
3. `turn2

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens the main menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- After trainer battles, overworld text may still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps.
- When a branch proves to be a dead end or rejoins known ground, record the rejoin point and rewind to a clean checkpoint near the branch point.
- In forest mapping, distinguish genuinely new topology from local loops; do not keep wiggling inside already-classified pockets.
- When a fresh branch contains both NPC lanes and grass, classify the safe road/NPC geometry first and postpone grass-heavy side tiles until after the obvious dry paths are mapped.
- Overworld signs in the forest can be annoying to clear cleanly; prefer a few single `B` presses after reading instead of long `A` mashes.
- When a checkpoint is already known to be grass-interruption-prone, favor one- or two-step dry-road probes and rewind immediately after learning a single local fact.
- In live wild-battle aftermath states (`Enemy X fainted!`), do not issue overworld plans yet; next turn should clear battle text first, then evaluate resulting overworld coords before deciding whether the branch is fresh.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian through Route 2 and Viridian Forest toward Pewter.
- Map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior.
- Avoid `boundary_t250` and `handoff_t250` as normal progress resets.
- The former best upper lead from `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible` has now collapsed: the `(27,26) -> (27,21) -> (31,15) -> (31,7)` family ultimately rejoins solved top-row and trainer-pocket geometry.
- Treat live `(30,18)` as a stale rejoin into the old trainer/sign-pocket family, not an active lead.
- Current live state on turn 440 is around `(16,40)` from the older `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh` family; this is now tertiary work only after the lower-area leads mostly collapsed.

## Key route notes
- Viridian north exit: from upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to Route 2 `(7,71)`.
- Viridian Pokecenter: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter. Nurse Joy is `(3,3)` facing up; choose `HEAL`.
- Route 2 south breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then north/right to expose the real path upward to the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse: from interior `MAP_0x32`, upper-left aisle to `(5,1)`, then `up` enters forest map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Entry from south gatehouse settles near `(17,47)`.
- Reproducible early route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then branch.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` challenges immediately.

### Former upper-corridor family is now stale
- From roughly `(27,34)`, the path through `(27,26)`, `(27,21)`, `(31,15)`, and `(31,7)` is real movement but **not** progression.
- Verified collapse points:
  - north/east continuation from `(31,15)` rejoins the solved top-row strip around `(28..22,1..2)`.
  - a different west/down branch from `(31,7)` eventually descends to live `(30,18)`, which is the old trainer/sign-pocket rejoin.
- Practical rule: do not spend live action turns from `(31,7)`, `(22,8)`, `(22,9)`, `(22,2)`, or `(30,18)`; rewind instead.

### Lower open lane into east rim and west-north offshoot
- Around `(25,27)`, walking south opens real lower-area continuation to `(25,33)` and beyond.
- Important progression chain so far:
  - `(25,27) -> (25,33)` opens the lower forest.
  - `(25,34) -> (27,38)` is a real lower-lane continuation.
  - From `(27,38)`, safe dry-road probing west/south found open hub `(22,42..43)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, an east-heavy dry-road probe reaches farther-east rim around `(30,41)` and `(32,41)`.
  - From `(23,43)`, a genuinely different west/north dry-road probe reaches `(21,40)`.
- Best checkpoints now:
  - `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`
  - `turn434_forest_28_40_retry`
  - `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`
  - `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`
  - `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`
  - `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`
  - `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`
  - `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`
  - `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`
  - `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`
  - `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`
  - `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`
  - `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`
  - `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`

### Practical forest rules
- Do **not** keep walking inside `(11..14,15..19)`.
- Do **not** spend action turns from live `(18,6)`, `(17,5)`, `(27,18)`, `(29,19)`, `(30,18)`, `(32,28)`, `(31,29..33)`, east-wall states around `(31,8)..(32,11)`, sign-lane wedge states around `(26,39)`, post-battle sign-row rejoin `(12,32)`, east-rim wedges `(32,41)` / `(31,40)`, or upper-family wedges `(22,8)` / `(22,9)` / `(22,2)`; rewind first.

## Battle heuristics
- Squirtle knows **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out to main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for white transition; sometimes two final `A` presses total are needed before RAM drops `battle` to none.
- After wild-battle wins, similar cleanup applies: clear `Enemy X fainted!`, EXP text, and any level/result text before assuming overworld control is back.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses.
- Consolidated wild-run rule in forest:
  1. intro text (`Wild X appeared!`) → `A`
  2. send-out text (`Go! A!`) / animation lag → wait, then single `A`
  3. visible 4-option menu → choose RUN explicitly
- If the screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet.
- If a cautious wild-run attempt lands in the move menu instead of the 4-option menu, use `B` once to recover before selecting RUN.
- If battle text looks partial or currently shows the enemy move line (e.g. `Enemy WEEDLE used STRING SHOT!`), assume it is still scrolling/resolving; use patience and single `A` presses rather than menu inputs.
- If the move menu appears after text lag, `B` then `down,right,A` is a reliable recovery to RUN.
- East-rim wilds have been interrupting path tests at `(30..32,41)`; after escaping, prefer checkpoint-based tiny probes over long mixed batches through grass.
- The same cautious wild-run recipe also handled Kakuna/Pikachu interruptions near the lower signfork loop around `(24..26,41)`; keep using skill-based phase identification instead of blind mashing.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for stepwise movement discovery.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement near grass/warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- `early_wild_run_opening` is preferred for battle-intro escape; `flee_wild_battle` is less reliable before the command menu is really ready.
- `paced_battle_progress` works well to finish slow battle text safely.
- When an apparent fresh family has already produced two different rejoin proofs, demote it aggressively in the playbook/todos instead of continuing to hope.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn434_forest_31_43_newedge`, `turn434_forest_28_40_retry`, `turn419_forest_27_34_retry`, `turn417_forest_18_35_retry2`, `turn409_forest_21_40_newlane`, `turn403_forest_29_41_grassedge`, `turn403_forest_28_40_npcside`, `turn397_forest_25_40_signfork`, `turn389_forest_32_41_eastrim`, `turn388_forest_30_41_eastrim`, `turn387_forest_23_43_openlane_start`, `turn381_forest_22_42_open_area`, `turn379_forest_25_34_lower_lane`, `turn377_forest_26_42_lower_area`, `turn376_forest_27_43_trainers_area`, `turn375_forest_27_34_trainers_visible`, `turn369_forest_18_35_signfork`, `turn365_forest_17_40_fresh`.
